Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The data looks numeric and time ordered so I need to identify patterns in the sensor readings that correspond to different manufacturing phases

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me start by looking at the overall structure There are 15 events spanning about a minute all from Machine M1 I notice the data seems to have clear transitions between different sensor profiles

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The first three events show low stable readings probably an idle state

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Then there is a sudden jump in temperature vibration and energy consumption from event 4 onward which suggests some active machining is starting

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at events 4 6 they show increasing temperature vibration and tool position movement with moderate material flow This could be a cutting operation where the tool is engaging with material

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Then events 7 8 have lower but still elevated readings with reduced flow maybe positioning or setup before a more intensive operation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 9 stands out dramatically with the highest temperature and energy but zero material flow

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Could be a welding operation where heat is applied without moving material

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Then events 10 12 show cooling down with some minor activity perhaps inspection or finishing

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Finally events 13 15 return to the initial idle state

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I should group these into clear phases and explain the rationale for each label based on the sensor patterns

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The key is to match sensor signatures to typical manufacturing operations high heat and vibration for cutting extreme heat without flow for welding etc

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Ill organize this chronologically and make sure the transitions between phases are logical
